mnemonics: S.O.B. - Sphere's Open Boundary: An Open Sphere does NOT include its boundary (like an open mouth), a Closed Sphere DOES include it (like a closed fist).
keyPoints: An open sphere S(x, r) is the set of all points y such that d(x, y) < r. It does not include the boundary.
A closed sphere S[x, r] is the set of all points y such that d(x, y) ≤ r. It includes the boundary.
The concepts of open and closed spheres are fundamental for defining open sets, closed sets, and neighborhoods in a metric space.
commonMistakes: Assuming an open sphere is a closed set. It is not; its complement is not necessarily open.
Confusing the definitions and using d(x, y) ≤ r for an open sphere or d(x, y) < r for a closed sphere.
Thinking the 'sphere' is always a round ball; its shape depends on the specific metric defining the space.
